name: Oxygen flow rate
description: Oxygen flow rate delivered to the patient, in L/min (distinct from FiO2 concentration).

concept.conceptName: Oxygen gas flow Oxygen delivery system

en.name: Oxygen flow rate
en.shortDescription: Oxygen flow rate delivered to the patient, in L/min (distinct from FiO2 concentration).
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Oxygen flow rate is the volume of oxygen delivered to a patient per unit time — the "litres per minute" set on a flowmeter feeding a nasal cannula, mask, or oxygen-delivery system. In source data this is a single numeric value per patient-timestamp. It is recorded in intensive care, on the ward, in anaesthesia, and in emergency settings for any patient on supplemental oxygen. It is reported in **litres per minute (L/min)** [1]. This is distinct from the fraction of inspired oxygen (FiO2), which is the *concentration* of oxygen in the inspired gas, not its flow (see Mapping Notes). ## Included Concepts The set lists the LOINC and SNOMED concepts for oxygen flow rate, grouped by source. The SNOMED delivered-flow concept is anchored with its descendants; the LOINC concepts are leaf terms listed explicitly. ### Delivered / inhaled oxygen flow (default) Use these for the flow of oxygen delivered to the patient when the source does not tie it to a specific delivery system. - *4141684 / 427081008 — Delivered oxygen flow rate (SNOMED)*: the flow of oxygen delivered to the patient; descendants are included. Synonym: "Fresh gas flow rate". - *44782836 / 698833004 — Delivered oxygen flow rate to non ventilated lung (SNOMED)*: delivered oxygen flow to the non-ventilated lung, e.g. during one-lung ventilation (a descendant of the concept above). - *3005629 / 3151-8 — Inhaled oxygen flow rate (LOINC)*: litres per minute of oxygen inhaled, for delivery devices that supply a volume per minute [1]. ### Oxygen delivery system flow - *3014080 / 19941-4 — Oxygen gas flow Oxygen delivery system (LOINC)*: oxygen gas flow measured on an oxygen delivery system. **Note on units**: all concepts are reported in **`8698 / L/min — liter per minute` (UCUM)**, the recommended unit for this set [1]. ## Mapping Notes - **Flow rate vs FiO2 — do not confuse them**: this set is the *flow* of oxygen in **L/min** (e.g. 2, 6, 15). The *fraction/concentration* of inspired oxygen (FiO2, in % or as a 0–1 fraction, e.g. 40% / 0.40) belongs to the Fraction of inspired oxygen (FiO2) concept set. A value like `10` with unit L/min is a flow rate; a value like `40` with unit % is a FiO2. Map each to its own set. - **Default concept** when the source records an oxygen flow rate without specifying the delivery system: *4141684 / 427081008 — Delivered oxygen flow rate*. - **Delivery-system flow**: map to *3014080 / 19941-4 — Oxygen gas flow Oxygen delivery system* only when the source explicitly attributes the flow to an oxygen delivery system. If unspecified, use the generic delivered-flow concept. - **One-lung ventilation**: map to *44782836 / 698833004 — Delivered oxygen flow rate to non ventilated lung* only when the source explicitly records flow to the non-ventilated lung. ## References [1] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 3151-8 — Inhaled oxygen flow rate. Available: https://loinc.org/3151-8/

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Add Oxygen gas flow Oxygen delivery system (3014080 / 19941-4); anchor delivered-flow SNOMED concept (4141684) with descendants. Resolves to 4. Added clinical description and recommended unit (L/min).
